name: Implement a token-bucket rate limiter
prompt:
Implement `createRateLimiter` in `src/rate-limiter.mjs` (currently a stub that throws). Contract:

`createRateLimiter({ capacity, refillPerSecond, now })` returns an object with a single method `tryRemove(count = 1)`.

- `capacity` (positive number): maximum tokens the bucket holds. The bucket STARTS FULL.
- `refillPerSecond` (non-negative number): tokens added per second, continuously (fractional accrual — after 0.5s at 2 tokens/sec, 1 token has accrued). The bucket never exceeds `capacity`.
- `now` (function, defaults to `() => Date.now() / 1000`): returns the current time in SECONDS. All time arithmetic must go through it.
- `tryRemove(count)`: if at least `count` tokens are currently available (after accruing refill since the last call), deduct them and return `true`; otherwise deduct NOTHING and return `false`. `tryRemove()` defaults to 1 token.
- `createRateLimiter` throws a `RangeError` if `capacity` is not a positive finite number or `refillPerSecond` is negative or not finite.

Keep the exported function name and signature unchanged. Do not add new dependencies.
